59.reason: This should be `else if` or the logic needs adjustment. If `num[i]` was 1, `i` is incremented. Then this `if` checks the NEW `i`. This skips checking if the pushed element's index (now current i) is 0? No, `i` was incremented inside the `if(num[i]==1)` block. So if we pushed, `i` points to the next element. Then `if(num[i]==0)` checks that next element. This logic is flawed because it processes the next element in the same iteration if it happens to be 0, skipping its own turn in the loop increment. It should be `else if`.
